title: 👑 Gauntlet A2 Elite ⭐⭐⭐⭐: The Final Trial

questions.prompt:
Reading — Read: "Last winter Youssef broke his leg while skiing. He stayed at home for a month and couldn't play football. His friends visited him every weekend."
Which sentence is TRUE?

options.text: Youssef hurt his leg last winter.

questions.explanation: The text says he "broke his leg" last winter, so (a) is true (to break your leg = to hurt it). It was his leg, not his arm (b); he "couldn't play football" (c is false); and his friends visited every weekend, so (d) contradicts the text.

options.text: We didn't go to the market yesterday.
She is reading a book at the moment.
They will going to move house next year.
He bought a new bike last week.

questions.explanation: The error is (c): you can't stack two futures — say "They are going to move" or "They will move", never "will going to". (a) is a correct past negative (didn't + base), (b) a correct present continuous (now), and (d) a correct irregular past (bought).

The error is (d): funny ends in consonant + y, so the y becomes i — funniest, not "funnyest". The correct ones follow their rules: hot doubles the t (hottest), easy changes y to i (easiest), and big doubles the g (biggest).

questions.prompt: Vocabulary — Choose the word that best completes the sentence: "The first question was easy, but the last one was really ___."
questions.explanation: "but" signals a contrast with easy, so the missing word is its opposite, difficult: easy ↔ difficult. Cheap, quiet and early are real adjectives, but none is the opposite of easy, so they break the contrast the sentence sets up.

questions.prompt: Grammar — Quantifiers: "There were ___ guests at the wedding, but only ___ of them danced."
questions.explanation: guests is countable and plural, so both gaps need countable quantifiers: many guests, and a few of them danced. So it is many / a few. much, a little and a great deal are for uncountable nouns and don't fit countable guests.

questions.prompt:
Reading — Read: "It's 7 o'clock in the morning. Lina is at the bus stop with her schoolbag. She is looking at her watch and the bus isn't here yet."
What is Lina probably doing?

options.text: She is playing in the park.
She is waiting for the bus.

questions.explanation: The clues — at the bus stop, with her schoolbag, looking at her watch, the bus not here yet — point to an action in progress now: she is waiting for the bus (d). "sleeping at home", "cooking breakfast" and "playing in the park" all contradict "is at the bus stop".
